roadmap: P8/A9_HEXA_LANG/HEXA-P8-1 — hexa 런타임 최적화 stage2 (top-10 병목 해결)

env.resolver: shared/scripts/bin/hexa (remote dispatch AG6; HEXA_LOCAL=1 forced local)

invocations.note: no staged hexa files → mostly boot

per_script_timing.bottleneck_class: subprocess_chain (git rev-parse + git diff --cached + thinking_log read + per-file exec awk/grep)

invocations.note: early-exit after help, represents bootstrap cost

per_script_timing.bottleneck_class: hashmap cache (n6_distance ~11만 calls), list push growth, string keying to_string(float)

invocations.note: includes verify_formulas subprocess 0.24s + ~5s awk/grep/sort chain on 18MB atlas.n6
cache_hit still runs orphan comm -23 pipeline + grade grep -oE + dup sort+uniq -d

per_script_timing.bottleneck_class: subprocess pipelines — awk/sort/comm on 18MB atlas.n6 (real CPU burned in awk+sort)
fast path — minimal work, bounded by exec("date +%s") + read_file log

interpreter_micro_bench._note: all measured locally on Mac, HEXA_LOCAL=1, single run

for_range_100k.vs_while: faster 1.53x per iter after amortizing iter construction

for_range_3k_iter.note: tiny — for-range iterator setup is cheap, scales linearly

map_insert_10000.complexity: super-linear — ratio 4.33x for 2x input, suggests O(n^1.5) or poor rehash

map_lookup_trycatch_hit_rate_100pct_20k.note: stage1 try/catch does catch exceptions expensively on miss

map_lookup_contains_key_hit_rate_50pct_1.vs_trycatch: try/catch faster 3.1x when HIT-dominant — CONTRADICTS pure-miss case

to_string_float_20k.note: ~38k ops/s — acceptable but hot in n6_distance cache key

top_10_bottlenecks.example_location: shared/blowup/core/blowup.hexa line ~1127 `_build_node_id_set` and many seed-list builders

top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: audit-only — interpreter.rs hashmap `.insert()` likely triggers rehash-every-N. stage2 proposal: double-and-copy growth with load_factor<=0.75
top_10_bottlenecks.proposal: PROPOSAL-C (binary-level, DO NOT execute this session)

top_10_bottlenecks.pattern: subprocess chain on large files (awk/grep/sort on 18MB atlas.n6)
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: extend atlas.n6.stats sidecar to also cache orphan_count/dup_count/grade_dist (miss → rebuild). Already has schema v1 (mtime/size/lines/nodes/edges/hubs). Bump v2.

top_10_bottlenecks.complexity: O(1) amortized but exception throwing ~50-100× base cost on miss
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: use contains_key only when miss-rate > 80%; keep try/catch when hit-rate > 50% (verified). DIAG-01 fix docs claim 60% hit → try/catch already optimal
top_10_bottlenecks.proposal: NO-OP — contradictory profile, leave as is (current choice correct for hit-dominant n6_distance)

top_10_bottlenecks.pattern: while i<N integer counter vs for x in range
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: rewrite `let mut i=0; while i<N { ... i=i+1 }` → `for i in 0..N { ... }` in counter loops

top_10_bottlenecks.pattern: to_string(float) as hashmap key — builds temporary string every call
top_10_bottlenecks.example_location: blowup.hexa:267 `let _key = to_string(value)` in n6_distance, called 110k+ times/run
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: binary: expose `Value::Float` as hashmap key directly (no string coercion). script workaround: quantize `to_int(value * 10000)` as cheaper key

top_10_bottlenecks.complexity: O(n) per call, O(n²) if used in loop
top_10_bottlenecks.example_location: shared/tool/lint.hexa:66 `fn _rfind` — manual reverse scan using `substring(i, i+1)`
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: interpreter gap: `last_index_of` builtin missing (P5 grammar). `str.rfind(ch)` would be O(n) but in Rust not hexa loop → ~30x faster

top_10_bottlenecks.pattern: repeated exec() subprocesses with shell parsing overhead
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: batch: one shell script with compound pipeline vs N hexa exec()s. blowup already did exec("printenv HOME") 15→1. Pattern needs to extend to lint's per-file cross-checks.

top_10_bottlenecks.cost_2k_invocations: CONJECTURE: small (<50ms) based on string-concat perf
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: interpreter: expose single-pass `json_escape` builtin. script: acceptable as-is (low total count)

top_10_bottlenecks.pattern: trace logging via append_file() per trace() call
top_10_bottlenecks.example_location: blowup.hexa:43 `_trace()` helper, ~100 trace points in a single run
top_10_bottlenecks.mitigation: buffer in-memory string, flush once at exit. Or gate behind BLOWUP_TRACE=1 properly (was observed empty trace file → perhaps default is off despite env, bug).

stage2_proposals.title: hexa-script patterns: range-map + mutating push + for-range loops
targets: blowup.hexa hot list builders (~45 push sites)
stage2_proposals.expected_gain: blowup.hexa one round: 2.4s → 1.7s estimated (CONJECTURE, needs empirical validation on full pipeline); list-build paths: up to 158x on pure-build patterns
stage2_proposals.risk: low — mutating .push() semantics must be verified; range.map restricted to pure transforms

stage2_proposals.title: atlas.n6.stats sidecar v2 — cache orphan_count/dup_count/grade_dist
stage2_proposals.risk: medium — cache invalidation on mtime change must cover orphan/dup as well (need HEAD hash already exists in schema v1); stale cache risk if .n6 edited in place without bump

stage2_proposals.title: hexa binary (USER APPROVAL REQUIRED): rehash policy + builtin fns
targets: HashMap insert: doubling-growth + load_factor 0.75 (fix O(n²) rehash)
hashmap Float key as first-class (to_string elimination)
stage2_proposals.expected_gain: blowup.hexa: cache-heavy phase ~5x; lint.hexa: ext_of fast-path ~30x
stage2_proposals.risk: HIGH — binary change affects all .hexa programs; must run regression suite
stage2_proposals.approval_gate: L0 — user explicit approval required before src/interpreter.rs edits

poc_results.verdict: range.map lambda 40x faster for pure transforms; push-mutate is drop-in 1.75x replacement for push-reassign at low risk. Recommended: scan blowup.hexa 45 push-reassign sites, apply mutate where idempotent, range.map where pure.

empirically confirmed: on warm atlas cache with matching mtime, parsing the sidecar JSON is 255x faster than re-running awk/grep/sort pipelines. miss-path unchanged. production path requires atlas_health.hexa cache-write to emit v2 schema on miss (4-5s one-off) and guarded v2-read on hit.

constraints_honored: audit + PoC only, no production changes to blowup/lint/atlas_health
next_session_hints: PROPOSAL-A can be applied by single-file Edit to blowup.hexa hot loops — 45 push-reassign sites. Needs empirical full-pipeline bench to confirm end-to-end gain.
PROPOSAL-B needs atlas.n6.stats schema migration + guard for stale orphan/dup values. Backward-compat read-v1 path.
PROPOSAL-C requires user approval to touch hexa-lang/src/interpreter.rs. Suggest rehash policy first (highest ROI, lowest regression risk).
